items.title: FOI documents shed light on appointment of controversial NT Administrator David Connolly
items.summary: Months before a furore over his social media posts, documents show the federal government completed background checks on controversial NT Administrator David Connolly.

items.title: Police investigate suspected drive-by shooting, firebombing in Melbourne's south-east
items.summary: Police are investigating multiple incidents overnight including a drive-by shooting, suspected firebombing and an allegedly violent attack on a tram.

items.title: Indonesian farmers improve food security while protecting Australia from citrus disease
items.summary: An Australian-funded project is educating Indonesian farmers about a devastating citrus disease that has spread to all major growing regions except Australia.

items.title: Seed swaps, preserved food and community gardens as preppers brace for uncertain future
items.summary: It may not be the end of the world, but these Australians are finding creative ways to beat the cost-of-living crisis and improve their food security in troubled times.

items.title: Indigenous kids training for life in and out of the ring at youth development camp in Canberra
items.summary: A Canberra Indigenous-led boxing camp is helping young fighters hone their skills for life inside and outside the ring in its first development camp, continuing a proud tradition of First Nations involvement in the sport.

items.title: Thirty years after losing his family in the Port Arthur massacre, Walter Mikac says gun control is still vital
items.summary: On April 28, 1996, a gunman killed 35 people at Port Arthur. Walter Mikac's wife and two daughters were among them. Thirty years on, he says gun control is as important as ever.

items.title: Ancient, critically endangered 'sterile' plant King's lomatia found for second time in Tasmanian wilderness
items.summary: A second population of an ancient tree, King's lomatia, has been found in the south-west of Tasmania. A scientist says the precise location must be kept secret, or risk being 'killed by love'.

items.title: Queensland's remote national park islands where you can volunteer and holiday for free
items.summary: North West Island is one of five national parks where you can holiday in exchange for campground hosting duties.

items.title: Predator-free farm helps endangered species thrive
items.summary: A predator-proof fence at a Victorian farm is helping endangered species recover while proving conservation and agriculture can successfully co-exist.

items.title: Ollie Hollands a late out for Carlton after Elijah Hollands's struggles, but coach Michael Voss says he is 'in a good space'
items.summary: Carlton coach Michael Voss says Elijah Hollands's younger brother Ollie is doing OK despite being a late out for the club's Anzac Day clash with Fremantle.

items.title: 'Tombstone tourists' keeping Australia's oldest surviving European cemetery alive in Parramatta
items.summary: Nestled between apartment blocks and opposite an escape room, St John's Cemetery holds many unique stories from history.

items.title: What happens to a car once it has been stolen and wrecked, and what if hazards are left inside?
items.summary: 'Opportunistic' car thieves are leaving vehicles littered with syringes, weapons and even traps, and in 2025, 77 per cent of vehicles inspected by one contractor had signs of methylamphetamine used at levels harmful to health.

items.title: Victoria's Moorabool River is struggling for water, but there is still life in it yet
items.summary: A small fish that smells like freshly-cut cucumber could be akin to a canary in the coalmine for one of Victoria's most stressed rivers.

items.title: Developer Assemble withdraws significant number of promised near-complete affordable homes
items.summary: Victorian planning authorities will allow Assemble to significantly reduce the number of affordable homes offered at large developments in Melbourne's north by more than half.

items.title: Licensed post office to remain in Dirranbandi after fears of closure
items.summary: Dirranbandi residents would have faced a two-hour car ride just to post a letter if it weren't for the work of these locals.

items.title: Dragons halfback Kade Reed to keep starting role, says interim coach Dean Young
items.summary: Dean Young backs Kade Reed to remain St George Illawarra's halfback even after the Dragons slumped to a 62-16 thrashing in the 20-year-old's highly anticipated NRL debut.

items.title: Long-held bitumen standards waived after supply caught in Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: Drivers could soon be travelling on roads built for US and European climates, as supply disruptions force governments to change the standard of bitumen used in Australia.

items.title: Gippsland footballer with Guillain-Barré syndrome returns to field after induced coma
items.summary: A Gippsland footballer who spent 15 days in an induced coma after contracting a rare nerve condition has made a remarkable return to the field.

items.title: Ensign restoration has WWI flag taking pride of place in Nowra museum
items.summary: A century-old red Australian flag that once led young men on a 100-kilometre march to enlist for World War I now takes pride of place in a museum in Nowra.

items.title: Live: Trump says US envoys waste too much time travelling, cancels Pakistan peace trip
items.summary: The US president says there has been "too much time wasted on travelling" by his officials, repeating suggestions the Iranian leadership remains unsettled. Follow live.

items.title: The grave adopters still caring for fallen Australian soldiers
items.summary: In The Netherlands, a quiet commitment to honour the young soldiers who were buried thousands of kilometres from home has continued for 80 years.

items.title: NDIS cuts show when Labor will move, gas tax shows when it won't
items.summary: Labor's radical transformation of the NDIS as it resists a growing chorus of support for a new gas tax exposes the limit of its willingness to act when given a social licence.

items.title: Former Treasury boss says Australians left with little to show from commodities boom
items.summary: Former Treasury secretary Ken Henry says the under-taxed resources sector has hollowed out Australia's economy, and raising more revenue from the sale of LNG resources was in the national interest.

items.title: Sex workers speak out about the HIV crisis in Papua New Guinea
items.summary: Workers have told the ABC about the battle to stay safe as the Pacific nation battles a rising number of infections.

items.title: Australia's lucrative drug market is fuelling a wave of narco subs crossing the Pacific
items.summary: At least seven narco subs have been discovered in Pacific waters in recent years, signalling a troubling new reality.

items.title: Prince Harry and Meghan's Australia trip is a contrast to King Charles's US state visit
items.summary: Little more than a week after Meghan and Harry's "quasi royal" tour in Australia, King Charles will be conducting the real thing in America, with much higher stakes.

items.title: Booing at Anzac Day services shows lack of public awareness of racism in Australia
items.summary: Jeers could be heard for more than a minute as Uncle Ray Minniecon spoke at yesterday's Anzac service in Sydney with similar scenes playing out in Melbourne and Perth.

items.title: Ringo Starr brought country music to The Beatles — now he's getting back to it
items.summary: Ringo's childhood love of classic country still influences the new music that The Beatles's drummer makes.

items.title: Calling all word wizards. Guy Montgomery's here with your Sunday spelling quiz
items.summary: Delighted by diagraphs or hyped for homophones? Step inside and showcase your spelling skills.

items.title: Today in history, April 26: Chernobyl's nuclear disaster, 40 years on
items.summary: Nuclear meltdown in 1986 reshaped ecosystems and human lives. Four decades on the legacy of the Chernobyl disaster is still unfolding.

items.title: Australian Olympian Gordon Ingate dies aged 100
items.summary: The Australian Olympian was still competing and winning into his 90s.

items.title: Russian attack on Ukrainian city of Dnipro kills seven, injures dozens
items.summary: An apartment block in the south-eastern city of Dnipro was struck twice by Russian drones, officials say.

items.title: Black Ferns outclass Wallaroos in Anzac Day Test

items.title: Captain Grant bristles at 'silly question' as Storm sink to historic low

items.title: How Henry the dachshund puppy survived three months lost in Perth's southern suburbs
items.summary: A dachshund puppy is reunited with its owner after being lost for almost three months, in an adventure spanning multiple suburbs.

items.title: AFL sacks appeals board chair Will Houghton after Lance Collard case
items.summary: Will Houghton KC is sacked as the chair of the AFL appeals board after the body's widely criticised statement and decision in the case of St Kilda player Lance Collard.

items.title: What Anzac Day means to some of the two million Australians who have served since Gallipoli
items.summary: Australians taking part in Anzac Day commemorations share stories of sacrifice and bravery, and what today means to them.

items.title: Nelly Korda streaks ahead in Chevron Championship second round as Hannah Green sneaks under the cut
items.summary: The Australian charge is struggling at the Chevron Championship in Texas, where world number two Nelly Korda is on fire after two rounds.

items.title: Cox's goal celebration sparks scuffle as Freo holds off Carlton comeback
items.summary: Mason Cox gets in the face of Ben Ainsworth after kicking a fourth-quarter goal, resulting in some fireworks as Fremantle wins its sixth consecutive match.

items.title: US boosts Iran war assets with third aircraft carrier USS George HW Bush

items.title: This easy 'hug in a bowl' chicken stew is perfect for midweek dinners - ABC News
items.summary: "It's a shortcut to that feeling that you get when you've slow cooked something all Sunday, but without that time or labour."

items.title: Booing during Welcome to Country at Melbourne, Sydney and Perth Anzac Day services draws condemnation
items.summary: Indigenous elders face racist booing from crowd members at dawn services in Melbourne, Sydney and Perth, with one man arrested and charged over his alleged involvement and others issued with move-on orders.

items.title: Australia's weather forecasts are suddenly identical each day
items.summary: Darwin to Melbourne, and everywhere in between, the seven-day forecasts reveal a monotonous similarity.

items.title: Bushfire survivors build fire-resilient house into Buchan hillside
items.summary: During the Black Summer bushfires, the Grahams watched their house burn from a peephole in a fire bunker. When they decided to rebuild, they created the most fire-resilient home possible. Here's how they did it.

items.title: Alex de Minaur beaten by Rafael Jodar in second round of Madrid Masters
items.summary: Alex de Minaur loses another early-round match at the Madrid Masters, going down to local teen Rafael Jodar in straight sets as his clay court season struggles.

items.title: Carlos Alcaraz out of French Open with wrist injury, Jannik Sinner urges him not to rush back
items.summary: Two-time defending champion Carlos Alcaraz will not be able to defend his titles at Roland Garros due to a wrist injury that has friend and rival Jannik Sinner concerned.

items.title: Penny the donkey marches in Mount Gambier's Anzac Day parade to honour animals that serve in war
items.summary: Penny the donkey, adorned in bright purple poppies, is spreading the Anzac Day legacy not just in Australia, but also in Cheshire in England.

items.title: Victorian government commits initial $860m for 7,000 extra social housing properties
items.summary: An extra 7,000 social housing properties will be built in Victoria in the coming decade, with an initial $860 million investment in the state budget, the Allan government has announced.

items.title: In search of the missing artist
items.summary: An Australian gallery has been selling sculptures as the work of an "internationally acclaimed" French artist. But the Paris backstory doesn’t stack up.

items.title: Why Anzac Day Test between Australia and New Zealand means so much to Wallaroos star Piper Duck
items.summary: Any time Piper Duck pulls on the Australian jersey is a special moment, but playing in the historic Anzac Day Test holds even greater significance for the Wallaroos back-rower.

items.title: Israel's killing of journalists in Lebanon shows a lack of guardrails on its actions
items.summary: According to the Committee to Protect Journalists, the Israel Defense Forces have committed more targeted killings of journalists than any other government's military since 1992.

items.title: The outcome of the Farrer by-election could be a watershed moment in Australian politics
items.summary: The Coalition's preference deals in Farrer could help fracture the Liberal Party's moderate base and invigorate dissatisfied voters.

items.title: Virginia Giuffre's legacy and the year that followed the Epstein files release

items.title: How the NDIS grew to four times the size that was expected
items.summary: An analysis of price forecasts in previous budgets reveals the NDIS was on track to cost more than four times what was initially expected.

items.title: With Mojtaba Khamenei a 'symbolic sovereign', who is running Iran?
items.summary: After a wave of assassinations targeting top Iranian officials and a supreme leader who has not been seen in public since the war began, a key question has emerged: who is really running Iran?

items.title: World War II veterans meet for first time eight decades after Borneo service
items.summary: Two of Australia's oldest veterans served in the same place at the same time during World War II, and after the fighting, both headed home to resume their lives in SA — but the 108-year-old and the 103-year-old had never met until recently.

items.title: Is Something For Kate frontman Paul Dempsey Australia's best cover artist?
items.summary: From Taylor Swift to Cher, Paul Dempsey's cover versions have been lauded. But does he have a secret weapon to making the perfect cover?

items.title: ABC 50/50 quiz: This week we're testing your knowledge of The Shawshank Redemption, hair metal and Bugs Bunny
items.summary: Every question has only two options: one is right, and the other is wrong. You have an even chance of guessing correctly each time.

items.title: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu treated for prostate cancer

items.title: US considers reviewing Falkland Islands sovereignty, suspending Spain from NATO over Iran war disapproval
items.summary: The email suggests the US may review the UK's claim to the Falkland Islands and Spain's NATO membership as punishment over their stance on the Iran war.

items.title: Australians with ISIS links leave Al-Roj camp in Syria to attempt journey to Australia
items.summary: A group of Australian families with ties to former Islamic State fighters have begun another attempt to travel home to Australia after spending years stranded in a Syrian refugee camp.

items.title: NSW star Kezie Apps says Women's State of Origin timing 'not ideal' after six-month lay-off for players
items.summary: Kezie Apps is one of a number of Origin stars who will play her first match in a matter of months when she represents the Blues, and she doesn't believe the lay-off is the best preparation.

items.title: Norway and Türkiye follow Australia with youth social media bans

items.title: India wants to redraw its electoral map for the first time in decades, but could this marginalise its southern states?

items.title: Why the gas tax momentum is building as Australia looks to properly cash in on its abundant natural resources
items.summary: Gas giants are breathing a sigh of relief after Prime Minister Anthony Albanese ruled out a 25 per cent tax on exports in next month's budget. But for how long will the federal government resist something that one leading expert says is in our "national interest"?

items.title: Failing on social mobility 'is inefficiency with a human face'
items.summary: Is reducing inequality and increasing social mobility one of Australia's best paths to improving our lacklustre economic productivity performance?

items.title: Man who helped fellow runner across Boston Marathon finish line says it was natural to help

items.title: The 10 best books out in April, featuring Patrick Radden Keefe and Caro Claire Burke
items.summary: This month's top picks include new works from Steve Toltz and Colm Tóibín, a satire inspired by Yellowface and an investigation into the death of a London teen who masqueraded as the son of a Russian oligarch.

items.title: Is it ever OK to use someone else's toothbrush?
items.summary: About half of us have shared a toothbrush — usually between spouses, or parents and children. We asked the experts if it's a good idea, even in an emergency.

items.title: ASX 200 down after Wall Street losses, oil prices spike again — as it happened
items.summary: Australia's ASX 200 has ended the week on a disappointing note, although the market clawed back most of its earlier losses to finish less than 0.1 per cent down for the day. Meanwhile, oil prices are rising again with Brent crude trading above $US105 a barrel. Look back on day's events with the ABC News markets blog.

items.title: Regional providers, families fear they will be hit hardest by cuts to NDIS
items.summary: People living with disability in regional areas already face extra hurdles in accessing support and families fear changes to the NDIS could have irreversible consequences.

items.title: Tasmania Police sergeant used dispatch system in 'relentless' monitoring of wife in family violence matter
items.summary: Text messages read in court set out how a Tasmania Police officer monitored his wife's movements, including via an internal dispatching system, as part of a 15-month family violence matter.

items.title: When is the Anzac Day public holiday? Which states have Monday off?
items.summary: Anzac Day is observed on April 25 every year. This year that date falls on a Saturday, but that doesn't mean a long weekend for everyone.

items.title: These fishermen in the South China Sea get paid for their time on water — not to fish
items.summary: Off China's southern coast, hundreds of fishing boats sit idle. The crews aren't there to fish, but rather to "maintain presence" as part of a shadowy, state-funded fleet.

items.title: Power price warning as proposed capital gains tax changes create 'uncertainty' for foreign investors
items.summary: The treasurer says changes to capital gains tax for foreign investors is a clarification, but experts say it could dampen investment.

items.summary: The Albanese government is reading the room when it comes to intergenerational equity ahead of the May 12 budget.

items.title: NDIS fraud crackdown will flip rules on their head for 'let it rip' provider market
items.summary: NDIS Minister Mark Butler will end the practice of handwritten invoices, providers without qualifications and a "let it rip" market of profiteering operators. But the sector warns changes must be navigated carefully.

items.title: Viva Energy boss defends fuel price surge, says more refineries needed for secure supply
items.summary: The Viva Energy refinery provides about 10 per cent of Australia's fuel. Its chief executive, Scott Wyatt, chats to Alan Kohler about the company's future in the wake of the Geelong fire.

items.title: How the new eligibility test for NDIS participants could work
items.summary: Labor's plan to create a new "assessment tool" to determine future NDIS eligibility based on functional need could backfire without the right settings, say experts.

items.title: Australia's world-leading Wedgetail surveillance planes coming up for replacement, Defence planning reveals
items.summary: Australia's fleet of world-leading surveillance aircraft is set to be replaced and analysts say the planes could be replaced by drones.

items.title: James Valentine, ABC talkback radio star, dies aged 64 after cancer diagnosis
items.summary: The presenter and professional saxophone player is being remembered for his ability to explore the humour and oddities of daily life on the airwaves.

items.title: Tough NDIS budget decision could prove the exception — or the template
items.summary: As far as tough budget decisions go, we haven't seen anything on this scale since Joe Hockey's ill-fated 2014 effort.

items.title: Labor's NDIS savings plan hinges on state-run supports
items.summary: Labor's plan to rein in NDIS growth will result in more than 160,000 people shifting off the scheme, with states expected to deliver alternative "foundational supports".

items.title: More than 3 million Australians over 65 to pay more for private health
items.summary: The federal government argues the move will help re-establish fairness across the generations.

items.title: How these households divide and conquer mealtimes
items.summary: From share houses to solo living, this is how three households manage the cooking differently.

items.title: Meme warfare and 'slopaganda' are defining the AI-era Iran war
items.summary: AI-generated Lego memes criticising the conflict in the Middle East and mocking Donald Trump are attracting a large audience online as a new front opens in the Iran war.

items.title: Papunya residents living in extreme heat sue NT government over unsafe housing
items.summary: Residents of the Central Australian community of Papunya say scorching outback summers are killing their people in unsafe housing. Now they’re suing the Northern Territory government to try to force change.

items.title: What 100 years of Australian soil samples in our national archive can tell us about our history and future
items.summary: Far from brown dirt, the Australian National Soil Archive houses 100,000 soil samples — some a century old — that represent a slice through time of the ground beneath our feet.

items.title: Whether it's grief or liberation, family estrangement affects many of us
items.summary: Our recent stories about the grief of family estrangement resonated strongly. Here's how some Australians have been affected by it.

items.summary: As Australia presses on to finally sign a key agreement with Vanuatu, reports of a security agreement with China have drawn a fierce response from Port Vila.

items.title: Pacific leaders declare emergency over potential fuel shortages

items.title: Health, climate and community sport: What older voters in the Tasmanian seat of Bass are talking about this election
items.summary: The federal seat of Bass is held by a margin of just 1.4 per cent. For these older voters who live in the electorate, health, climate and community sport are the issues that will decide their vote.

items.title: Mobile coverage is an election issue in regional areas. What's been promised?
items.summary: For many regional and remote Australians, reliable mobile coverage can be life-saving. They are desperate for both sides of politics to take meaningful action towards eliminating black spots.

items.title: Tiwi Islanders eager to have their say as remote voting for the federal election begins
items.summary: Voters in the small remote community of Pirlangimpi on the Tiwi Islands, north of Darwin, have become some of the first to cast their votes for the federal election.

items.title: Energy projects in South West WA threaten votes for major federal parties
items.summary: Australia's two major parties have tied their federal election fates to controversial energy projects in WA's South West where the issues have polarised voters.

items.title: Cost of living, housing among key concerns in SA marginal seat of Boothby
items.summary: At this year’s federal election, the South Australian seat of Boothby is one of the state’s two marginal seats. As part of the ABC’s “Your Say” campaign, we spoke to residents in the electorate about their biggest concerns.

items.title: Melbourne seat of Calwell highlights Labor woes as Gaza pain galvanises Muslim voters
items.summary: Dissatisfaction with the Albanese government's stance on Israel-Palestine among many Arab and Muslim voters threatens to unseat Labor MPs, in some places for the first time.

items.title: Next-generation voters call for better engagement ahead of federal election

items.title: Housing advocates seek federal funding election promise for co-op model
items.summary: Advocates admit co-operative housing models are not for everyone but say for the right residents, they offer more security and affordable pricing than traditional rentals.

items.title: Graduates in 'debt vortex' as HECS front and centre for first time in an election

items.title: Australia's growing cohort of tenants fear they will rent forever

items.summary: Voters tell ABC's Your Say campaign they are struggling more than ever with housing costs.

items.title: Push for seniors' dental scheme grows ahead of federal election
items.summary: Imagine living in aged care and not having your teeth or dentures properly cleaned and checked each day. That's a risk faced by elderly Australians, prompting calls for the next federal government to boost oral health training and services.

items.title: Voters tell ABC's Your Say they want politicians with a vision, not bickering
items.summary: Voters tell ABC's Your Say campaign they want courage and vision from politicians, not bickering.

items.title: Have Your Say: What was the issue, policy or event that decided your vote?
items.summary: After a five-week campaign, the results are in with Labor returned to government. Is there a policy or issue that swayed your vote? Have Your Say.

items.title: Reserve Bank restructure closer to reality after Treasurer Jim Chalmers offers olive branch to Angus Taylor
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of Australia is a step closer to its biggest restructure in three decades after the treasurer offers an assurance to his Coalition counterpart.

items.title: The US election is about style more than substance. It's why Kamala Harris needs to nail her DNC speech in about eight seconds
items.summary: When the Democrat presidential candidate Kamala Harris delivers her speech to the Democratic National Convention today, style will be almost everything.
